Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The value '50 months (IQR 39–63)' is explicitly stated in the 'Findings' section on page 1: 'Median follow-up was 50 months (IQR 39–63).'

Control Arm.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states that patients were 'randomly assigned to receive to ADT (...) alone or in combination with docetaxel'. The 'Findings' section on page 1 further clarifies that '193 to receive ADT alone' was one of the allocated groups, serving as the comparison group without the experimental docetaxel.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states that patients were 'randomly assigned to receive to ADT... alone or in combination with docetaxel'. The 'Findings' section on page 1 further specifies '192 patients were randomly allocated to receive ADT plus docetaxel and 193 to receive ADT alone', clearly indicating two treatment arms.

Total Participants - N.evidence: The 'Findings' section on page 1 states: 'Between Oct 18, 2004, and Dec 31, 2008, 192 patients were randomly allocated to receive ADT plus docetaxel and 193 to receive ADT alone.' The 'Results' section on page 3 also confirms: 'Between Oct 18, 2004, and Dec 31, 2008, we enrolled 385 patients (figure 1).' The total number of participants is 192 + 193 = 385.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: In the 'Methods' section on page 1, it states: 'They were randomly assigned to receive to ADT (orchiectomy or luteinising hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with non-steroidal antiandrogens) alone or in combination with docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously on the first day of each 21-day cycle; up to nine cycles).' Treatment Arm 1 is 'ADT plus docetaxel', which combines androgen-deprivation therapy (hormonal therapy) and docetaxel (chemotherapy).
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: ADT (orchiectomy or luteinising hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with non-steroidal antiandrogens) in combination with docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously on the first day of each 21-day cycle; up to nine cycles)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'They were randomly assigned to receive to ADT (orchiectomy or luteinising hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with non-steroidal antiandrogens) alone or in combination with docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously on the first day of each 21-day cycle; up to nine cycles).' This describes the two treatment arms, with the combination therapy being Treatment Arm 1.

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'They were randomly assigned to receive to ADT (orchiectomy or luteinising hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with non-steroidal antiandrogens) alone or in combination with docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously on the first day of each 21-day cycle; up to nine cycles).' The 'Findings' section on page 1 further clarifies: '192 patients were randomly allocated to receive ADT plus docetaxel and 193 to receive ADT alone.'

Control Arm - N.evidence: The 'Findings' section on page 1 states: '193 to receive ADT alone'. Figure 1 'Trial profile and patient disposition at data cutoff' on page 3 shows '193 allocated to receive ADT alone'. Table 1 'Baseline characteristics' on page 3 also lists 'ADT alone (n=193)'. Table 2 'Treatments at progression' on page 4 and Figure 2 'Kaplan-Meier curves for overall survival by treatment group' on page 4, and Figure 3 'Kaplan-Meier curves for PSA progression-free survival (A) and clinical progression-free survival (B) by treatment group' on page 5 all confirm 'ADT alone' arm started with 193 participants.

NCT.evidence: Found in the 'Methods' section on page 1: 'This trial is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov, number NCT00104715.'

PubMed ID.evidence: PubMed ID is not explicitly reported in the document.

Trial Name.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy alone or with docetaxel in non-castrate metastatic prostate cancer (GETUG-AFU 15)
Trial Name.evidence: Found as the main title of the article on page 1.

Author.evidence: Found in the author list on page 1: 'Gwenaelle Gravis, Karim Fizazi, Florence Joly, Stéphane Oudard, Franck Priou, Benjamin Esterni, Igor Latorzeff, Remy Delva, Ivan Krakowski, Brigitte Laguerre, Fréderic Rolland, Christine Théodore, Gael Deplanque, Jean Marc Ferrero, Damien Pouessel, Loïc Mourey, Philippe Beuzeboc, Sylvie Zanetta, Muriel Habibian, Jean François Berdah, Jerome Dauba, Marjorie Baciuchka, Christian Platini, Claude Linassier, Jean Luc Labourey, Jean Pascal Machiels, Claude El Kouri, Alain Ravaud, Etienne Suc, Jean Christophe Eymard, Ali Hasbini, Guilhem Bousquet, Michel Soulie'.

Year.evidence: Found in the journal information on page 1: 'Lancet Oncol 2013; 14: 149-58' and 'Published Online January 8, 2013'.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is a multi-page article from 'The Lancet Oncology' journal, indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: Found in the title of the article on page 1: 'a randomised, open-label, phase 3 trial' and in the 'Methods' section: 'In this randomised, open-label, phase 3 study'.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The 'Interpretation' section on page 9 states: 'As far as we are aware, ours is the first phase 3 trial to have assessed docetaxel chemotherapy in patients with metastatic non-castrate prostate cancer.', indicating it is an original report.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Findings' section, it states: 'Between Oct 18, 2004, and Dec 31, 2008, 192 patients were randomly allocated to receive ADT plus docetaxel and 193 to receive ADT alone.' The experimental arm is 'ADT plus docetaxel' with 192 participants. This is further confirmed by Figure 1 on page 3, which shows '192 allocated to receive ADT plus docetaxel'.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The 'Procedures' section on page 2 states: 'Quality of life was assessed with the self-administered European Organisation for Research and Treatment of Cancer quality-of-life questionnaire C30.' The 'Results' section on page 6 further reports: 'At baseline, mean quality-of-life scores were similar in the two groups... Mean scores were significantly lower in the group given ADT plus docetaxel than in that given ADT alone during the treatment phase... However, no differences in mean global and functional scores were recorded between the two groups at 12 months...'

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly stated in the title of the article: 'Androgen-deprivation therapy alone or with docetaxel in non-castrate metastatic prostate cancer (GETUG-AFU 15): a randomised, open-label, phase 3 trial'.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1: Baseline characteristics, under 'Age (years)' for 'ADT plus docetaxel (n=192)', which is the treatment arm. The value is 63 (57-68), where 63 is the median age.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1: Baseline characteristics, under 'Age (years)' for 'ADT alone (n=193)', which is the control arm. The value is 64 (58-70), where 64 is the median age.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document describes the treatment regimens and the number of docetaxel cycles, but it does not explicitly state the median duration in months that participants in the treatment arm received on-treatment therapy. The 'median length of time from randomisation to subsequent chemotherapy' and 'median time to subsequent treatment' are reported, but these are distinct from 'on-treatment duration'.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document describes the treatment regimens, but it does not explicitly state the median duration in months that participants in the control arm received on-treatment therapy. The 'median length of time from randomisation to subsequent chemotherapy' and 'median time to subsequent treatment' are reported, but these are distinct from 'on-treatment duration'.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: Found in Table 1, row 'Metastatic at diagnosis', column 'ADT plus docetaxel (n=192)' on page 3. This represents synchronous metastases in the treatment arm.
Found in Table 1, row 'Metastatic at diagnosis', column 'ADT alone (n=193)' on page 3. This represents synchronous metastases in the control arm.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: Found in Table 1, row 'Metastatic after treatment for local disease', column 'ADT plus docetaxel (n=192)' on page 3. This represents metachronous metastases in the treatment arm.
Found in Table 1, row 'Metastatic after treatment for local disease', column 'ADT alone (n=193)' on page 3. This represents metachronous metastases in the control arm.

Quality of Life Scale.value: European Organisation for Research and Treatment of Cancer quality-of-life questionnaire C30 (EORTC QLQ-C30). Baseline global scores: ADT plus docetaxel 67.4 (20.2); ADT alone 65.4 (21.9). Mean quality-of-life scores at 12 months: ADT plus docetaxel 67.62 (18.4); ADT alone 66.36 (20.2).
Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The 'European Organisation for Research and Treatment of Cancer quality-of-life questionnaire C30' is explicitly mentioned on page 3 under 'Methods' as the tool used to assess quality of life. Table 1 on page 3, 'Baseline characteristics', reports 'EORTC QLQ-C30 global scores' as 67.4 (20.2) for the ADT plus docetaxel group and 65.4 (21.9) for the ADT alone group. The 'Results' section on page 6 further details mean quality-of-life scores at baseline and at 12 months, stating 'At baseline, mean quality-of-life scores were similar in the two groups (ADT plus docetaxel: 67·41 [SD 20.2]; ADT alone 65.38 [21.9]; p=0.405; appendix)' and 'no differences in mean global and functional scores were recorded between the two groups at 12 months (67.62 [18.4] vs 66.36 [20.2]; p=0.696)'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: The document provides baseline characteristics in Table 1, including 'Metastatic at diagnosis' and 'Metastatic after treatment for local disease', and 'Glass prognostic group'. However, it does not explicitly categorize patients into 'High' or 'Low' volume/burden of metastatic disease for the treatment arm (ADT plus docetaxel).

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: The document provides baseline characteristics in Table 1, including 'Metastatic at diagnosis' and 'Metastatic after treatment for local disease', and 'Glass prognostic group'. However, it does not explicitly categorize patients into 'High' or 'Low' volume/burden of metastatic disease for the control arm (ADT alone).

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: The document provides baseline characteristics in Table 1, including 'Metastatic at diagnosis' and 'Metastatic after treatment for local disease', and 'Glass prognostic group'. However, it does not explicitly categorize patients into 'High' or 'Low' volume/burden of metastatic disease for the control arm (ADT alone).

Primary Endpoint(s).value: overall survival (ie, time between randomisation and death from any cause)
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'Methods' section, it states: 'The primary endpoint was overall survival.' On page 2, under 'Procedures', it further clarifies: 'The primary endpoint was overall survival (ie, time between randomisation and death from any cause).'

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: time to clinical progression or death (clinical progression-free survival; cPFS) and time to PSA progression, clinical progression, or death (biochemical progression-free survival; bPFS)
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 2, under the 'Methods' section, the text states: 'The primary endpoint was overall survival (ie, time between randomisation and death from any cause). Secondary endpoints were time to clinical progression or death (clinical progression-free survival; cPFS) and time to PSA progression, clinical progression, or death (biochemical progression-free survival; bPFS).'

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1 'Baseline characteristics' on page 3, under 'Initial Gleason score*' for 'ADT plus docetaxel (n=192)'. Summed values for '2-6' (18 [10%]) and '7' (66 [35%]) to get 84 (45%).

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1 'Baseline characteristics' on page 3, under 'Initial Gleason score*' for 'ADT alone (n=193)'. Summed values for '2-6' (14 [7%]) and '7' (64 [34%]) to get 78 (41%).

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: On page 4, in the paragraph under Figure 1, it states: "Doses were reduced in 21 (11%) of the 189 patients who received docetaxel." This indicates that 189 patients in the ADT plus docetaxel group received docetaxel. The total number of patients allocated to this arm was 192 (Figure 1). The percentage is calculated as (189/192) * 100 = 98.4%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: On page 4, in the paragraph under Figure 2, it states: "120 (62%) of the 193 patients allocated to receive ADT alone subsequently received docetaxel at the time of progression of castration-resistant prostate cancer". This indicates that 120 patients (62%) in the control arm received docetaxel during the trial (at progression).

PS - N (%) | 0 | Treatment.evidence: The document provides 'Karnofsky score 100% (90-100)' as median (IQR) for the ADT plus docetaxel (Treatment) arm in Table 1, and mentions 'Karnofsky score of at least 70%' as an eligibility criterion in the Methods section. While Karnofsky score is related to Performance Status, the exact number and percentage of participants with a Performance Status (PS) score of 0 is not explicitly reported. Figure 4 uses 'ECOG performance status 0' as a category but does not provide N (%).

PS - N (%) | 0 | Control.evidence: The document provides 'Karnofsky score 100% (90-100)' as median (IQR) for the ADT alone (Control) arm in Table 1, and mentions 'Karnofsky score of at least 70%' as an eligibility criterion in the Methods section. While Karnofsky score is related to Performance Status, the exact number and percentage of participants with a Performance Status (PS) score of 0 is not explicitly reported. Figure 4 uses 'ECOG performance status 0' as a category but does not provide N (%).

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Treatment.evidence: The document states 'Karnofsky score of at least 70%' as an eligibility criterion in the Methods section, which corresponds to ECOG PS 0 or 1, meaning no patient should have a PS of 2 or higher. Table 1 provides 'Karnofsky score 100% (90-100)' as median (IQR) for the ADT plus docetaxel (Treatment) arm. Figure 4 uses 'ECOG performance status 1-2' as a category but does not provide N (%). The exact number and percentage of participants with a Performance Status (PS) score of 1-2 is not explicitly reported.

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Control.evidence: The document states 'Karnofsky score of at least 70%' as an eligibility criterion in the Methods section, which corresponds to ECOG PS 0 or 1, meaning no patient should have a PS of 2 or higher. The exact number and percentage of participants with a Performance Status (PS) score of 1-2 is not explicitly reported.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a randomized controlled trial and includes a systematic review of other trials, but it does not provide an overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT) as a general statement or a summary of such judgments.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The study population included patients with 'metastatic at diagnosis' (synchronous metastases), accounting for 67% and 75% of the treatment groups (Table 1, page 3). The results were reported by 'prognostic groups' (Good, Intermediate, Poor) for the overall patient cohort (page 5, Figure 4), which included these synchronous patients.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The study population included patients who were 'Metastatic after treatment for local disease' (metachronous metastases), accounting for 32% and 24% of the treatment groups (Table 1, page 3). The results were reported by 'prognostic groups' (Good, Intermediate, Poor) for the overall patient cohort (page 5, Figure 4), which included these metachronous patients.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The 'prognostic groups' (Good, Intermediate, Poor) were defined on the basis of several criteria, including 'appendicular versus axial disease' (page 1, Introduction). This distinction is commonly used to differentiate between low and high volume metastatic disease. The results were reported by these prognostic groups (page 5, Figure 4).

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The 'prognostic groups' (Good, Intermediate, Poor) were defined on the basis of several criteria, including 'appendicular versus axial disease' (page 1, Introduction). This distinction is commonly used to differentiate between low and high volume metastatic disease. The results were reported by these prognostic groups (page 5, Figure 4).

Treatment Class.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) alone or in combination with docetaxel
Treatment Class.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Methods' section, it states: 'They were randomly assigned to receive to ADT (orchiectomy or luteinising hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with non-steroidal antiandrogens) alone or in combination with docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously on the first day of each 21-day cycle; up to nine cycles).'

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The title of the article is "Androgen-deprivation therapy alone or with docetaxel in non-castrate metastatic prostate cancer". The 'Background' section states: "We investigated the effects of the addition of docetaxel to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) for patients with metastatic non-castrate prostate cancer." The 'Methods' section further clarifies: "They were randomly assigned to receive to ADT... alone or in combination with docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously on the first day of each 21-day cycle; up to nine cycles)."

Type of Therapy.value: ADT (orchiectomy or luteinising hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with non-steroidal antiandrogens) alone or in combination with docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously on the first day of each 21-day cycle; up to nine cycles).
Type of Therapy.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'They were randomly assigned to receive to ADT (orchiectomy or luteinising hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with non-steroidal antiandrogens) alone or in combination with docetaxel (75 mg/m² intravenously on the first day of each 21-day cycle; up to nine cycles).'

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: On page 4, in the last paragraph of the 'Results' section, it states: '3-year overall survival was 64.2% (95% CI 57.5-71-6) in the group given ADT plus docetaxel'.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: On page 4, in the last paragraph of the 'Results' section, it states: '3-year overall survival was ... and 62.9% (56-3-70-2) in the group given ADT alone'.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: On page 5, in the first paragraph under 'Articles', it states: 'Similarly, median cPFS was significantly longer in the group given ADT plus docetaxel (23.5 months, 20.5-31.9)'.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: On page 5, in the first paragraph under 'Articles', it states: 'Similarly, median cPFS was significantly longer in the group given ADT plus docetaxel (23.5 months, 20.5-31.9) than in the group given ADT alone (15.4 months, 12-5-19.8)'.

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Treatmen.evidence: The document does not report median PFS specifically for patients with 'high volume metastasis (by CHAARTED criteria)' in the experimental arm. While prognostic groups are mentioned, they are not explicitly defined as CHAARTED high/low volume, and median PFS values for these subgroups are not provided.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The document reports median progression-free survival (PFS) in months and hazard ratios (HR) for the comparison between arms, but does not provide a specific "PFS Rate (%) | Overall" as a percentage for the treatment arm alone. Median biochemical PFS (bPFS) for ADT plus docetaxel was 22.9 months (95% CI 19.6-28.4) and median clinical PFS (cPFS) was 23.5 months (20.5-31.9) (page 5). The hazard ratio for bPFS (ADT plus docetaxel vs ADT alone) was 0.72 (95% CI 0.57-0.91) and for cPFS was 0.75 (95% CI 0.59-0.94) (page 5), which is a comparative measure, not a rate for a single arm.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The document reports median progression-free survival (PFS) in months and hazard ratios (HR) for the comparison between arms, but does not provide a specific "PFS Rate (%) | Overall" as a percentage for the control arm alone. Median biochemical PFS (bPFS) for ADT alone was 12.9 months (11.9–17.7) and median clinical PFS (cPFS) was 15.4 months (12-5-19.8) (page 5). The hazard ratio for bPFS (ADT plus docetaxel vs ADT alone) was 0.72 (95% CI 0.57-0.91) and for cPFS was 0.75 (95% CI 0.59-0.94) (page 5), which is a comparative measure, not a rate for a single arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Found in 'Findings' section on page 1: '72 serious adverse events were reported in the group given ADT plus docetaxel'. Serious adverse events are typically Grade 3 or higher. The safety population for ADT plus docetaxel is 189 (Table 3 header). Calculated as 72/189 * 100 = 38.1%.
Found in 'Findings' section on page 1: 'No serious adverse events were reported in the ADT alone group'. The safety population for ADT alone is 186 (Table 3 header). Calculated as 0/186 * 100 = 0%.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: Table 3 lists 'Toxic effects reported in the first 6 months of treatment' which are treatment-related. It provides N (%) for 'Grade 3-4' and 'Grade 5' for individual adverse events. However, an aggregate number and percentage of patients experiencing any treatment-related adverse event of Grade 3 or higher is not provided in the document.

Table 3 lists 'Toxic effects reported in the first 6 months of treatment' which are treatment-related. It provides N (%) for 'Grade 3-5' for individual adverse events. However, an aggregate number and percentage of patients experiencing any treatment-related adverse event of Grade 3 or higher is not provided in the document. The text on page 6 states 'No severe adverse events were reported in the ADT alone group', but this is not an aggregate for all Grade 3 or higher treatment-related events.

Found in 'Findings' section on page 1: 'Four treatment-related deaths occurred in the ADT plus docetaxel group'. Grade 5 adverse events are deaths. This is confirmed on page 6, first paragraph. The safety population for ADT plus docetaxel is 189 (Table 3 header). Calculated as 4/189 * 100 = 2.1%.

Found in 'Findings' section on page 1: 'No serious adverse events were reported in the ADT alone group', which implies no Grade 5 events. This is further supported by 'No severe adverse events were reported in the ADT alone group' on page 6, first paragraph, and all Grade 5 entries in Table 3 for the control arm being 0. The safety population for ADT alone is 186 (Table 3 header). Calculated as 0/186 * 100 = 0%.
